title: Publications
text:
GIS Analyst
February 2024 – August 2025
Hamilton, Canada

Create, maintain, update, manipulate, and analyze data in the City’s corporate GIS database.
Complete spatial analysis and GIS application development projects with functional business units to support decision-making.
Assisted Enterprise specialists with publishing services to ArcGIS Online and maintaining those services.




Developed and managed in-house projects for the forestry department, supporting field technicians with essential tools and resources.
Led a project on Spongy Moth Egg Masses, creating Field Smart forms and dashboards to improve data collection and analysis.
Conducted woodland assessments focused on tree species identification and health evaluation.






GIS Analyst
April 2022 – February 2024
Waterloo, Canada

Gathered and compiled geographic data from a variety of sources, including satellite imagery, aerial photographs, field observations, and existing maps, ensuring data accuracy and reliability.
Created and customized maps and visualizations using ArcMap, ArcPro, and Adobe Illustrator to effectively communicate spatial information.




Contributed to several pivotal projects that supported company growth, including remediation projects for U.S. mining clients and initiatives associated with a 5% increase in client acquisition.
Developed specialized expertise in emergency response (ER) initiatives related to oil spills, derailments, and wildfires.





Developed an automated FME (Feature Manipulation Engine) workspace to systematically divide Excel-based field data according to updated data requirements, improving efficiency across field data collection workflows.
Designed and deployed a diverse set of surveys using Survey123 Connect, configuring field maps with essential layers to optimize usability and streamline data collection for field personnel.






GIS Technician
October 2021 – April 2022
Uxbridge, Canada

Contributed to a hazardous tree risk assessment and prioritization project for Parks Canada.
Used Trimble Catalyst and Survey123 to locate and document hazardous trees using high-accuracy GPS.
Conducted field data collection using Trimble Catalyst and populated tree attribute information in ArcGIS.
Prioritized trees based on condition and likelihood of future hazard occurrence.

title: Publications
text:
Peer-reviewed publications:
Despite listing them on your resume, it may also be pertinent to create a seperate tab for publications and reports. As your career progresses, this list may become quite long, so be sure to organize things. You may want to point to ‘most recent’ publications, or categorize things by project/topic.

title: Project 1 - Capstone Deliverables
Project 1 - Capstone Deliverables
Content & Deliverables
text:
This is a sample page where you can archive project deliverables. Every header you use will correspond to a tab in the legend - shown on the right-hand side of your screen (“On this page”).
If you’ve produced any GIS layers for your 599 project, this may be a good place to share them as interactive maps through leaflet. Alternatively, you can upload high-res figures showing correlations, box-plots, etc. from your report here, and provide a brief overview of key project results. If you have written some useful functions, you may also want to share those here to demonstrate proficiency in particular coding languages or synergies between notable coding packages.

title: Pritty Arattu's E-Portfolio
text:
Master’s Thesis Project

Natural Resources Canada
August 2025 – May 2026
Vancouver, BC

Conducted Individual Tree Segmentation (ITS) on airborne LiDAR point cloud data from the Petawawa Research Forest using multi-temporal ALS datasets (three time periods).

Implemented and compared multiple ITS algorithms (Watershed, Dalponte2016, and Li2012) to evaluate algorithm performance across time series.

Documented code and workflows to support project reproducibility and long-term scalability.

title: Pritty Arattu
text:
I’m a geomatics professional, with a background in geology and a deep-rooted curiosity about maps and landscapes.
After completing a postgraduate diploma in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) at Sir Sandford Fleming College, I’ve worked across public and private sectors such as Parks Canada, GHD, and the City of Hamilton, supporting environmental analysis and spatial decision-making.
Currently, I’m pursuing the Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management (MGEM) at UBC, where I’m focusing on individual tree segmentation using air borne LiDAR data with Natural Resource Canada.
Outside of work, I enjoy reading, biking, and staying active. Ideally somewhere near trees!

title: Get to Know Me
text:
Get to Know Me
I’m a geomatics professional with a background in geology and a deep-rooted curiosity about maps and landscapes.
After completing a postgraduate diploma in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) at Sir Sandford Fleming College, I’ve worked across public and private sectors including Parks Canada, GHD, and the City of Hamilton, supporting environmental analysis and spatial decision-making.
Currently, I’m pursuing the Master of Geomatics for Environmental Management (MGEM) at UBC, focusing on individual tree segmentation using airborne LiDAR data with Natural Resources Canada.
Outside of work, I enjoy reading, biking, and staying active — ideally somewhere near trees.
